methods.description: Preparation and observation of pollen samples using Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) to study the ultrastructure of Heliconia pollen grains.

steps.description: Unopened flowers were collected from plants in the field or greenhouse and fixed in formalin-acetic acid-alcohol (FAA) for at least 24 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and fix unopened flowers in FAA

steps.description: The anthers were dissected from the flowers, taken through the standard acetolysis preparation and mounted in glycerine jelly for observation with phase microscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: dissect anthers and prepare for phase microscopy

steps.description: Critical point drying was employed to reduce surface tension distortion of the sporoderm.
steps.searchable_summary: perform critical point drying on pollen

steps.description: The anthers were opened and the dried pollen shaken out onto a glass coverslip overlaid by a thin film of polyvinyl chloride adhesive.
steps.searchable_summary: mount dried pollen on glass coverslip

methods.description: Preparation and observation of Heliconia pollen samples using Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM) to study internal structures.

steps.description: Entire flowering bracts were harvested and the individual flowers subtended by each bract were sequenced and labeled according to size.
The anthers were dissected from each flower and fixed. Critical ontogenetic studies depended on routine fixation with 4% glutaraldehyde.
steps.searchable_summary: dissect and fix anthers with glutaraldehyde

steps.description: Post-fixed for 1 hour at 20°C in 2% OSO4 in 0.1 M phosphate buffer.
The material was again rinsed in buffer, dehydrated, and then infiltrated with epoxy resin.
steps.searchable_summary: rinse, dehydrate, and infiltrate anthers with epoxy resin

steps.description: Sections were cut on a Cambridge-Huxley ultramicrotome with a DuPont diamond knife.
Ultrathin sections were post-stained with saturated uranyl acetate in 50% ethanol followed by 0.2% lead citrate.
steps.searchable_summary: post-stain sections with uranyl acetate and lead citrate

steps.description: Photomicrographs were taken on a Siemens Elmiskop 101 TEM.
Thick sections (0.5-1 µm) for viewing with brightfield microscopy were stained in a drop of toluidine blue dissolved in 1% aqueous solution of borax.
steps.searchable_summary: stain thick sections with toluidine blue

steps.description: Permount was used as the mounting medium for permanent slides of the pollen sections.
steps.searchable_summary: mount sections on slides with Permount
